definitions.definition: To a great extent or degree.

parsedExamples.example: Not very many (of them) had been damaged.
She's so very similar to her mother.
In the end, the tickets didn't turn out so very expensive.

examples: Not very many (of them) had been damaged.
She's so very similar to her mother.
In the end, the tickets didn't turn out so very expensive.

definitions.definition: Conforming to fact, reality or rule; true.
Used to firmly establish that nothing else surpasses in some respect.

parsedExamples.example: He was the very best runner there.

examples: He was the very best runner there.

definitions.definition: True, real, actual.

parsedExamples.example: the fierce hatred of a very woman
the very blood and bone of our grammar
We're approaching the very end of the trip.

examples: the fierce hatred of a very woman
the very blood and bone of our grammar
We're approaching the very end of the trip.

definitions.definition: The same; identical.

parsedExamples.example: He proposed marriage in the same restaurant, at the very table where they first met.
That's the very tool that I need.
The very man I wanted to see!

examples: He proposed marriage in the same restaurant, at the very table where they first met.
That's the very tool that I need.
The very man I wanted to see!

definitions.definition: With limiting effect: mere.

parsedExamples.example: The very idea of climbing the ladder brings me out in a sweat.
The very idea/thought!

examples: The very idea of climbing the ladder brings me out in a sweat.
The very idea/thought!

definitions.definition: enslaved
alternative form of ferien
